meta.title: Construction Waste Utilization Case Study | WEI LAN
meta.description: Review the WEI LAN construction waste utilization line reference, including case description, equipment scope, site details, buyer background, and video reference.

bodyHtml:
C&D Waste Case Detail

Construction waste utilization line

Reference for an integrated construction waste recycling line producing recycled aggregate and separated recoverable materials.

Reference Timeline

From landfill pressure to recoverable output streams

The case is ordered by buyer evaluation flow: background, cooperation unit, constraints, solution, and before/after comparison.

01

Project background

Construction and decoration waste needed utilization value

The project processes mixed construction and decoration waste into reusable output streams instead of routing the material directly toward landfill pressure.

Material family
2 streamsconstruction and decoration waste
Output direction
3 outputsaggregate / metal / light material
02

Cooperation unit

Anonymized regional construction waste utilization company

The cooperation unit was expanding aggregate recovery and needed a referenceable line that buyers could compare by waste type, site conditions, and equipment scope.

Operator type
1 regional teamwaste utilization company
Review focus
4 checksmaterial / site / scope / video
03

Real constraints

Outdoor yard logistics shaped the line arrangement

The layout had to account for loader feeding, equipment foundation area, aggregate stockpiles, metal collection, and separated light material handling.

Site areas
5 areasfeed to stockpile
Primary limit
1 yardoutdoor logistics footprint
04

Solution

70 t/h Lion One line with integrated separation

The equipment scope combines feeding, crushing, screening, magnetic separation, air separation, conveying, dust-control interfaces, and output collection.

Capacity reference
70 t/hLion One line
Core processes
4 processescrush / screen / magnet / air
05

Before / after comparison

The reference package shows a clearer recovery route

BeforeMixed site material with low routing clarity

Construction and decoration waste required multiple handling steps before recoverable fractions were visible.

AfterIntegrated process from feeding to stockpiles

The video package covers feeding, crushing, screening, magnetic separation, air separation, and final output piles.

Video scope
6 stepsfeeding to output
Request Reference Package Back to Case Studies
